rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SU HWA WAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The request to maintain investments is not reflected in the petition caption or included in the prayer. Schedule F of the accounting is incorrect. The invoice for the payment to Always Best Care on 2/4/19 might need to be filed, as there was also a payment on 1/14/19, presumably for the week of services from 1/7/19 - 1/14/19. Attorney costs are not fully itemized (date, payee, description, amount). The Court might inquire into estate planning considerations. (The income yield reported in paragraph 9 of the petition to be from the Fidelity funds also includes the nominal interest from the two bank accounts.) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BEYER FAMILY REMAINDER ANNUITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Reformation As To Distributive Provisions Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Do the petitioners, who are the settlors, but are not trustees or beneficiaries of this irrevocable trust, have standing to bring this petition under Probate Code section 17200(a) which provides that a trustee or beneficiary of a trust may petition the court concerning the internal affairs of the trust? 2) Article I-E of the trust excluded private foundations from the settlors' choices of permissible charitable remainder beneficiaries. The drafting attorney who happens to be the trustee of the subject trust admits that, before the trust was prepared, the settlors communicated to the attorney their desire to name both public charities and private foundations as remainder beneficiaries. The petition alleges that Article I-E was a product of the drafting attorney's error caused by lack of experience in the area of relevant tax law and should be reformed to add private foundations as permissible remainder beneficiaries. Were the income tax deductions reported by the petitioners on their 2016 tax returns calculated based on the current tax-favored language of the trust or on the less tax-favored language of the trust proposed by this petition? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELIDE S. BEAL TRUST DATED 18, 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/2021. 30 days' notice must be given on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/11/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONY JER-FU LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/21 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: STILL NO COURTESY COPIES as of 9/17/21 despite this having been posted in the tentative ruling. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] A skim of the petition online indicates that the persons named as the executors in the will were not listed in item 8 of the petition and did not receive (or waive) notice. Not all waivers of bond appear to have been fully executed. A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] Courtesy copies of all papers, including any papers filed to address these issues, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/1/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY QUINTANA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A. and B., and 14.60 E.] No proof of service. A quick skim of the papers online as a one-time courtesy indicates that the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties & Liabilities is incomplete. The will says she is divorced, but the petition indicates that there was a deceased spouse? The deceased spouse is not identified in item 8 of the petition with the date of death. And if a decedent is survived by no known next of kin, the petitioner must file a declaration, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Courtesy copies of all previously filed papers and any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/1/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
